excluded_defs.name: collectAssertionError
excluded_defs.reason: Investigated to understand error collection mechanism, but the fix doesn't touch AbstractSoftAssertions

exploration_log.aha_moment: The standard pattern throughout the codebase is to use `throwAssertionError(errorMessageFactory)` inherited from `AbstractAssert`, rather than directly calling `Failures.instance().failure(info, ...)`. RecursiveComparisonAssert was the exception, deviating from this pattern.

dead_ends.why_irrelevant: The fix doesn't require changing AbstractSoftAssertions; the proxy mechanism already handles collection once throwAssertionError is used

exploration_log.hindsight: I would read more AbstractAssert subclasses first to understand the throwAssertionError convention before diving into the proxy mechanism.

key_decisions.reasoning: Both methods had the same pattern inconsistency; fixing both makes the codebase consistent

search_sequence.result: ErrorCollector.intercept wraps proxied method calls in try-catch for AssertionError; throwAssertionError is the virtual method pattern that ensures consistent routing

search_sequence.reasoning: Verify the fix path is correct
search_sequence.result: isEqualTo/isNotEqualTo are intercepted by ERROR_COLLECTOR; the fix ensures consistent method-level failure routing

queries.query_text: RecursiveComparisonAssert isEqualTo soft assertions failure not collected, fix by using throwAssertionError instead of direct Failures throw in assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/api/RecursiveComparisonAssert.java

solve_notes: Changed `RecursiveComparisonAssert.isEqualTo` and `isNotEqualTo` to use `throwAssertionError(errorMessageFactory)` (inherited from `AbstractAssert`) instead of directly calling `throw objects.getFailures().failure(info, ...)`. This routes the failure through `AbstractAssert.throwAssertionError`, which is the standard pattern in the codebase and ensures the soft assertion proxy's `ErrorCollector` can intercept and collect the error rather than it propagating uncaught.
task_text:
Fix SoftAssertions not collecting errors from recursive comparison assertions

In `assertj-core/src/main/java/org/assertj/core/api/SoftAssertions.java`,
when using `assertThat(obj).usingRecursiveComparison().isEqualTo(expected)`
inside a `SoftAssertions` block, the recursive comparison failure is thrown
immediately instead of being collected. The `AbstractAssert.isEqualTo` path
for recursive comparison bypasses the soft assertion proxy error collection
in `AbstractSoftAssertions`. Fix the recursive comparison flow in
`AbstractAssert` to route failures through `SoftAssertions`'s
`AssertionErrorCollector` instead of throwing directly via `Failures`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: generateProxyClass
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: confirms which methods are intercepted by ERROR_COLLECTOR vs PROXIFY_METHOD_CHANGING_THE_OBJECT_UNDER_TEST, preventing wrong assumptions about proxy setup

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum_sufficient set has the two methods being edited and the throwAssertionError target. An AI agent would additionally want to read ErrorCollector.intercept and SoftProxies.generateProxyClass upfront to understand WHY the change is needed (i.e., confirm the proxy catches thrown AssertionErrors and that throwAssertionError is the intended path). Without these, the agent might spend time wondering whether the proxy already handles direct throws (it does in theory), and might doubt whether the change is actually necessary.
